task_completion.description: Open the Electrical Engineering Calculations app
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Electrical Engineering Calculations app interface is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Phase/Line voltage conversion section
task_completion.visual_evidence: The screen displays a calculator specifically designed for three-phase systems, Phase Voltage, or Line Voltage conversions.
The value '480' is entered into the input field designated for Line Voltage or V_LL.
The app displays the calculated Phase Voltage (V_ph) result, which should be approximately 277.1 Volts (between 276.1 and 278.1).

task_completion.description: Save a screenshot of the result
task_completion.visual_evidence: The trajectory shows a screenshot being captured of the final calculation result (e.g., screenshot animation, notification, or the final image itself is the required screenshot).

integrity.description: The agent used the Electrical Engineering Calculations app rather than a generic calculator or web browser
integrity.visual_evidence: The UI elements match a dedicated electrical engineering application, not a standard OS calculator or a web search page.

integrity.description: The agent did not type the expected answer into a text editor or notes app
integrity.visual_evidence: The inputs and results are displayed within the app's native input/output fields, not in a plain text editing interface.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to echo the result
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal emulators or command-line interfaces used to generate the output.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input Line Voltage (V_LL) is 480V. The expected Phase Voltage (V_ph) is 277.1V (calculated as 480 / √3). Acceptable values are within a tolerance of ±1.0V (276.1V to 278.1V). The final screenshot must capture this result.
